sourceMovie.title: One Piece: Curse of the Sacred Sword
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto One Piece: Curse of the Sacred Sword (2004) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: One Piece: Stampede
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If One Piece: Curse of the Sacred Sword clicked for you, One Piece: Stampede (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and adventure, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after One Piece: Curse of the Sacred Sword?
faq.answer: Try One Piece: Stampede, One Piece Film: Z, One Piece Film: GOLD—each shares DNA with One Piece: Curse of the Sacred Sword while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is One Piece: Curse of the Sacred Sword?
faq.answer: One Piece: Curse of the Sacred Sword (2004) is best described as Animation, Adventure, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is One Piece: Curse of the Sacred Sword part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "One Piece Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
